extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long high plateau (values ≈0.8–0.9) occupying the middle of the series, with lower baseline (≈0.45–0.55) both before and after the plateau.  
R2: At one side of the long plateau there is an additional, much shorter opposite excursion (either a narrow spike above baseline or a narrow dip below it).  
R3: The series starts and ends close to the same baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is a single long high plateau in the middle of the series, where values rise sharply from baseline (≈0.47–0.50) to ≈0.8–0.9 and stay almost flat.  
R2: Before the rise and after the fall, the signal stays near the same baseline level (no additional spikes or dips).  
R3: The maximum value occurs only within that central plateau segment.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a long low plateau in the middle of the series, where values drop sharply from baseline (≈0.47–0.50) to a much lower level (≈0.06–0.2) and stay almost flat.  
R2: Before and after this long low plateau, the signal returns to the baseline level.  
R3: On one side of the long low plateau there is an additional narrow, deeper dip below the low-plateau level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long plateau clearly above the baseline level (high segment) between roughly indices 40 and 100.  
R2: Outside this high segment, the signal stays near a mid-level baseline (around 0.45–0.5) except for at most one short, sharp spike.  
R3: There is no long, wide low “valley” segment that stays clearly below the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long plateau clearly above the baseline (high segment) between roughly indices 50 and 100.  
R2: Before the high segment (indices 0–50) the signal stays near a mid-level baseline (around 0.47–0.5) with only small noise.  
R3: After the high segment (indices 100–150) the signal returns to the same mid-level baseline as before, with no additional strong spikes or valleys.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long plateau clearly below the baseline level (low segment) between roughly indices 40 and 90.  
R2: Before and after this long low segment, the signal returns to a higher baseline level, but within indices 100–140 there is at least one short, sharp deep valley much lower than the long low segment.  
R3: There is no long, wide sustained high plateau above the baseline similar to Classes 0 or 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium level around 0.45–0.55.  
R2: There is one long plateau at a higher level (around 0.8–0.9) occupying roughly the central part of the series.  
R3: On one side of this high plateau there is a brief opposite excursion (either a short deep dip or a short extra spike), but only on one side, not both.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium level around 0.45–0.55.  
R2: There is a single long plateau at a higher level (around 0.8–0.9) in the central part of the series.  
R3: Outside this central high plateau (before it starts and after it ends) the signal remains roughly flat, with no pronounced spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends at a medium level around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: There is one long, deep low plateau (around 0.05–0.2) in the central part of the series.  
R3: In addition to this long central low plateau, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip on one or both sides of it, going noticeably below the medium level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long plateau at a high level (around 0.75–0.85) between roughly indices 30–100.
R2: Outside this plateau, the baseline is at a medium level (around 0.45–0.55).
R3: On one side of the plateau (beginning or end), there is an isolated narrow spike going clearly higher than the plateau before quickly returning to the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long plateau at a high level (around 0.7–0.9) between roughly indices 50–100.
R2: Before this plateau, the signal stays around a medium baseline (about 0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R3: After this plateau, the signal returns to about the same medium baseline without any pronounced spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long plateau at a high level (around 0.75–1.0) roughly in the central part of the series.
R2: On exactly one side of this plateau (left or right), there is a deep, narrow dip where the value drops clearly below the surrounding baseline (toward ~0.15–0.25).
R3: Away from the plateau and dip, the signal stays at a medium baseline (around 0.45–0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long interval in the middle where values are clearly lower than at the beginning and the end (a wide, deep valley between two higher plateaus).  
R2: Near one end of the series there is a short, pronounced peak that rises well above the surrounding plateau.  
R3: The values before and after the valley stay roughly at the same medium level (similar starting and ending plateau height).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long interval in the middle where values are clearly higher than at the beginning and the end (a wide, tall plateau between two lower regions).  
R2: The beginning and ending parts of the series stay at approximately the same lower level.  
R3: There are no additional sharp peaks or dips outside the central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long interval in the middle where values are clearly lower than at the beginning and the end (a wide, deep valley between two higher plateaus).  
R2: The values before and after this central valley are approximately equal and higher than the valley (similar starting and ending plateau height).  
R3: In addition to the central valley, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip near one side of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long plateau in the middle segment (roughly indices 40–100) whose values are clearly above the surrounding baseline.  
R2: There is a single, narrow spike that briefly exceeds this plateau, located either before or after the plateau.  
R3: Outside the plateau and spike, the signal stays close to a constant baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long, central plateau (roughly indices 40–100) with values clearly above the rest of the series.  
R2: There are no additional pronounced spikes or dips outside this plateau (the rest of the series stays near a flat baseline).  
R3: The baseline level before and after the plateau is roughly symmetric and remains within a narrow amplitude band.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a long central segment where values are clearly below the surrounding baseline (a wide “valley” between about indices 40–100).  
R2: There is one additional, narrow dip that goes even lower than this valley, located near the end of the series.  
R3: Outside the valley and the deep dip, the signal remains near a higher, approximately constant baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series spends a long central segment (roughly middle third of time) clearly above the baseline level, forming a wide “high plateau”.  
R2: At least one of the two ends (early or late in time) stays close to the baseline level, distinctly lower than the central high plateau.  
R3: There may be one additional short, opposite excursion (a brief dip if the main pattern is high, or a brief spike if the main pattern is low) located near one of the ends, but it is much shorter than the main central segment.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single wide “high plateau” in the middle, well above the baseline.  
R2: Both beginning and end of the series remain near the same baseline level, lower than the central plateau.  
R3: Apart from noise, there are no extra pronounced spikes or dips outside the single central plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long central “low valley” (middle segment) that is clearly below the baseline level.  
R2: Both beginning and end of the series stay near a higher baseline, above the central valley.  
R3: There is at least one brief, sharp opposite excursion (a narrow deep dip) near the start or the end that is significantly shorter in duration than the central valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau in the middle of the series (roughly indices 50–100) where the value is clearly lower than the baseline (around 0.2–0.3 vs baseline ≈0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is a single tall spike above the baseline (value ≥0.8) that occurs near exactly one edge of the long low plateau (either just before it or just after it).  
R3: Outside the spike and the low plateau, the series stays close to a flat baseline around 0.45–0.5.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long high plateau in the middle of the series (roughly indices 50–100) where the value is clearly higher than the baseline (around 0.8–0.9 vs baseline ≈0.47–0.5).  
R2: Both before and after this high plateau, the signal returns to about the same flat baseline level (≈0.47–0.5).  
R3: No additional distinct spikes or dips appear outside this single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long low plateau (around 0.05–0.1) between two high baseline segments, typically in the middle third of the series.  
R2: In addition to the long low plateau, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip (value much lower than the surrounding baseline) located near one side of the long low plateau.  
R3: Outside the low plateau(s) and short dip(s), the series stays near a higher, roughly flat baseline (≈0.45–0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one wide “low” plateau in the middle (values clearly below the baseline for a long interval) and is at a higher, roughly constant baseline level at both the start and the end.  
R2: On exactly one side of this wide low plateau (either before it or after it) there is a short, narrow peak that rises above the baseline.  
R3: There is no long “high” plateau (sustained segment above the baseline) anywhere in the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a lower, roughly constant baseline level.  
R2: After some time it makes a sharp transition to a wide, nearly constant “high” plateau above the baseline, then later makes a sharp transition back down.  
R3: There are no additional narrow peaks or deep dips outside this single wide high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one wide “low” plateau in the middle (values clearly below the baseline for a long interval) and is at a higher, roughly constant baseline level at both the start and the end.  
R2: In addition to this wide low plateau, there is exactly one short, narrow deep dip that goes even lower than the plateau (either just before or just after the wide low region).  
R3: There is no long “high” plateau (sustained segment above the baseline) anywhere in the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long plateau at a low level (around 0.2–0.25) in the middle section (roughly indices 50–100).
R2: There is a short, prominent peak (value > 0.7) located near the right side of the series (roughly indices 110–130).
R3: Outside the plateau and peak, the signal stays around a mid level (~0.45–0.5) at the beginning and at the very end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long plateau at a high level (around 0.8–0.9) in the middle section (roughly indices 50–100).
R2: Before and after this plateau, the signal remains around a mid level (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R3: The series does not contain any sharp isolated spikes far above or below those two levels.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long plateau at a very low level (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle section (roughly indices 40–100).
R2: The signal is around a mid level (~0.45–0.5) both before and after this low plateau.
R3: There is one additional short, deep dip (value < 0.2) located near the left side (roughly indices 15–30), below the mid-level baseline.
